Generally, log-based anomaly detection can be performed using either point, collective, or contextual approaches. In the log-based anomaly detection literature, most of the existing studies propose either a contextual or collective approach, where the decision is taken by examining a sequence of log events. However, detecting abnormalities in log data can be performed using a point-based approach, where one single log record is the only feature needed. Therefore, several studies explored utilising a point-based approach to detect anomalous events on logs data, such as in operating systems , , drone devices , and distribution systems , . source_paragraph: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

source_paragraph: VII. THE COUPLING BETWEEN CC-OFDM MULTI-BEAMS VII. THE COUPLING BETWEEN CC-OFDM MULTI-BEAMS A. THE COUPLING BETWEEN NEIGHBORING BEAMS A. II. LITERATURE STUDY
A. Neo4j as Graph Database
Neo4j is a popular graph database widely used for managing
and querying complex relational data. One of the commonly
used command languages for Neo4j is Cypher Query Lan-
guage (CQL). Cypher provides an intuitive and expressive syn-
tax for interacting with graph data, allowing users to perform
various operations such as searching, adding, updating, and
deleting data within the Neo4j graph database."]

source_paragraph: can be fine-tuned into our dataset. V. CONCLUSION V. CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
